document.title: in-situ BTO thin film growth with PLD
document.abstract: We investigated ferroelectric and crystalline properties of as-grown heteroepitaxial BaTiO3 thin films, that are consequently grown on different single crystalline substrates in a single vacuum cycle by Pulsed Laser Deposition (PLD) technique in O2 atmosphere.

datasets.description: For many years, ferroelectric (FE) materials have been in focus of scientific and technological investigations for their unique optoelectronic, photorefractive, piezoelectric and nonlinear optical properties which are based on their spontaneous electric polarization and polarization reversal by an external electric field [1,2]. These features lead to versatile applications in electronic devices such as non-volatile memory cells (FRAM) or tunable capacitors, as well as in sensors, optical beam modulators, higher harmonic generators, etc. Another important application for ferroelectrics is the possible use as a memristor in the form of a ferroelectric tunnel junction [3]. Many of these applications rely on the manufacturing of a few nm thin, homogeneous, ferroelectric films between capacitor plates. While the switching behavior of bulk ferroelectrics is well understood, the physical effects at the nano-scale are a matter of ongoing research. In particular, at film thicknesses less than the typical diameter of the FE domain nucleus the nucleation-limited mechanism may be replaced by a homogeneous switching [4,5].\\nPreviously, we investigated heteroepitaxial Pt and BaTiO3 layers are consequently grown on a single crystalline MgO(001) substrate in a single vacuum cycle by Pulsed Laser Deposition (PLD) technique in O2 atmosphere (1e-2 mbar). As grown heteroepitaxial BaTiO3(100) films were monodomain ferroelectrics with the spontaneous polarization direction perpendicular to the surface of the film, as revealed by piezoresponse force microscopy. To further improve the structural quality of and subsequently to minimize the leakage paths across as grown BaTiO3 films, the top electrodes they were subjected to post-annealing in oxygen at the atmospheric pressure to the top electrode formation. The resistivity across thus the prepared FE films with 20 nm and 50 nm in thickness is are R > 5e5 Ohm/cm2 [6,7].\\n\\n[1]\\tV. Ya. Shur, P. S. Zelenovskiy, Micro- and nanodomain imaging in uniaxial ferroelectrics: Joint application of optical, confocal Raman, and piezoelectric force microscopy, J. Appl. Phys. 116(6), 066802 (2014). \\u202810.1063/1.4891397\\n[2]\\tA. V. Ievlev, A. N. Morozovska, E. A. Eliseev, V. Y. Shur, S. V. Kalinin, Ionic field effect and memristive phenomena in single-point ferroelectric domain switching, Nat. Commun. 5, (2014).\\u2028 \\u202810.1038/ncomms5545\\n[3]\\tE. Y. Tsymbal, A. Gruverman, Beyond the barrier, Nat. Mater. 12(7), 602 (2013). 10.1038/nmat3669\\u2028\\n[4]\\tJ. Junquera, P. Ghosez, Critical thickness for ferroelectricity in perovskite ultrathin films, Nature 422, 506 (2003). \\u202810.1038/nature01501 \\n[5]\\tM.J. Highland, T.T. Fister, M.-I. Richard, D.D. Fong, P.H. Fuoss, C. Thompson, J.A. Eastman, S.K. Streiffer, G.B.Stephenson, Polarization Switching without Domain Formation at the Intrinsic Coercive Field in Ultrathin Ferroelectric PbTiO3, Phys. Rev. Lett. 105, 167601 (2010)\\u2028. 10.1103/PhysRevLett.105.167601

Many of these applications rely on the manufacturing of a few nm thin, homogeneous, ferroelectric films between capacitor plates. datasets.description: For many years, ferroelectric (FE) materials have been in focus of scientific and technological investigations for their unique optoelectronic, photorefractive, piezoelectric and nonlinear optical properties which are based on their spontaneous electric polarization and polarization reversal by an external electric field [1,2]. These features lead to versatile applications in electronic devices such as non-volatile memory cells (FRAM) or tunable capacitors, as well as in sensors, optical beam modulators, higher harmonic generators, etc. Another important application for ferroelectrics is the possible use as a memristor in the form of a ferroelectric tunnel junction [3]. Many of these applications rely on the manufacturing of a few nm thin, homogeneous, ferroelectric films between capacitor plates. While the switching behavior of bulk ferroelectrics is well understood, the physical effects at the nano-scale are a matter of ongoing research. In particular, at film thicknesses less than the typical diameter of the FE domain nucleus the nucleation-limited mechanism may be replaced by a homogeneous switching [4,5].\\nPreviously, we investigated heteroepitaxial Pt and BaTiO3 layers are consequently grown on a single crystalline MgO(001) substrate in a single vacuum cycle by Pulsed Laser Deposition (PLD) technique in O2 atmosphere (1e-2 mbar). As grown heteroepitaxial BaTiO3(100) films were monodomain ferroelectrics with the spontaneous polarization direction perpendicular to the surface of the film, as revealed by piezoresponse force microscopy. To further improve the structural quality of and subsequently to minimize the leakage paths across as grown BaTiO3 films, the top electrodes they were subjected to post-annealing in oxygen at the atmospheric pressure to the top electrode formation. The resistivity across thus the prepared FE films with 20 nm and 50 nm in thickness is are R > 5e5 Ohm/cm2 [6,7].\\n\\n[1]\\tV. Ya. Shur, P. S.
